content.problemStatement:
Sushil went to a sweet shop because he always craves to eat sweets. This is not good for his health as he has diabetes, but he doesn't care. A laddu costs Rs. 101010 while a jalebi costs Rs. 202020.

Initially, Sushil had Rs. XXX but then Sushil bought NNN laddus, how many jalebis can he buy with the remaining money?


Input Format

The first line of input contains XXX and NNN - the initial amount of money Sushil had and the number of laddus he bought already.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the the number of jalebis he can buy.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Sushil had Rs. 999999 but after spending on buying 333 laddus, he only has Rs. 696969 left, which is enough to buy only 333 jalebis.

Explanation:
Sushil had Rs. 100100100 but he spent all of it on buying laddus. So, he can buy 000 jalebis.
